tossups.question: A woman of this first name writes an advice column under the name “Mrs. Green Thumb” and is born despite her mother never knowing she was pregnant; that woman is the protagonist of Carol Shields’s The Stone Diaries. In an opening scene set in Switzerland, a woman with this first name remarks on the number of hotels nearby and dismisses complaints about being unable to get “American candy” made by her younger brother Randolph, who later falls asleep and causes a (*) boat ride to be cancelled. Another woman with this first name repeats “You always look so cool” to confess her love to a man who attended St. Olaf College, and says of her daughter that “the best thing a girl can be” is a “beautiful little fool.” For 10 points, give this first name shared by a Henry James title character who dies of “Roman Fever” and the wife of Tom Buchanan in The Great Gatsby.
tossups.answer: Daisy [accept Daisy Goodwill Flett; accept Daisy Miller; accept Daisy Buchanan; prompt on Annie (but roll your eyes because the novella is literally called Daisy Miller)]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Daisy [accept Daisy Goodwill Flett; accept Daisy Miller; accept Daisy Buchanan; prompt on Annie (but roll your eyes because the novella is literally called Daisy Miller)]
tossups.question_sanitized: A woman of this first name writes an advice column under the name "Mrs. Green Thumb" and is born despite her mother never knowing she was pregnant; that woman is the protagonist of Carol Shields's The Stone Diaries. In an opening scene set in Switzerland, a woman with this first name remarks on the number of hotels nearby and dismisses complaints about being unable to get "American candy" made by her younger brother Randolph, who later falls asleep and causes a (*) boat ride to be cancelled. Another woman with this first name repeats "You always look so cool" to confess her love to a man who attended St. Olaf College, and says of her daughter that "the best thing a girl can be" is a "beautiful little fool." For 10 points, give this first name shared by a Henry James title character who dies of "Roman Fever" and the wife of Tom Buchanan in The Great Gatsby.

bonuses.leadin: In this story, a woman recounts how her grandmother died after being sent into the “deadly cold” to collect flowers for her sister Harriet. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In this story, a woman recounts how her grandmother died after being sent into the "deadly cold" to collect flowers for her sister Harriet. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this story in which Mrs. Ansley recounts the death of her grandmother from the title disease. This story ends with the revelation to Mrs. Slade that a rendezvous in a certain city resulted in the birth of her daughter Barbara.
This author of “Roman Fever” wrote stories such as “Autres Temps” and “The Other Two,” as well as the novels The House of Mirth and The Age of Innocence.
This title character is warned of Roman Fever by Frederick Winterbourne in a novella by a different author where she visits the Colosseum with Giovanelli.
parts_sanitized: Name this story in which Mrs. Ansley recounts the death of her grandmother from the title disease. This story ends with the revelation to Mrs. Slade that a rendezvous in a certain city resulted in the birth of her daughter Barbara.
This author of "Roman Fever" wrote stories such as "Autres Temps" and "The Other Two," as well as the novels The House of Mirth and The Age of Innocence.
This title character is warned of Roman Fever by Frederick Winterbourne in a novella by a different author where she visits the Colosseum with Giovanelli.
answers: Daisy Miller [or Daisy Miller] (Daisy Miller is by Henry James.)
answers_sanitized: Daisy Miller [or Daisy Miller] (Daisy Miller is by Henry James.)

bonuses.leadin: This author wrote of Basil Miller and Olive Chancellor in The Bostonians. For ten points each:
parts: Name this American-British author who used his heritage to contrast the two sides of the Atlantic in works such as The Portrait of A Lady.
James often told his stories through these characters whose descriptions cannot be trusted by the reader. Other examples of these characters include Humbert Humbert and Holden Caulfield.
In this Henry James novella, the title character is courted by Winterbourne, who finds her sitting in the center of the Colosseum with Giovanelli.

bonuses.leadin: The protagonist catches malaria after a trip to the Colosseum with Giovanelli. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this novella, in which the title character meets Winterbourne and eventually dies of “Roman Fever.”
This author of Daisy Miller wrote about Isabel Archer in The Portrait of a Lady and about Christopher Newman’s failed attempt to woo Claire in The American.
In this Henry James novella, Mrs. Grose tells the tale of siblings Miles and Flora and their encounter with ghosts of the former housekeeper Miss Jessel and her lover Peter Quint.

bonuses.leadin: An unnamed governess cares for Miles and Flora in one of his works. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author of The Turn of the Screw.
Chadwick Newsome leaves Madame Marie de Vionnet, and Lewis Strether returns to America at the end of this Henry James novel.
In this Henry James novella, Winterbourne is rejected by the title character, who dies of “Roman fever” after Giovanelli takes her to the Colosseum.
